title: Evangelion Original
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Original             Quote Post by bph » Fri Jul 15, 2005 2:07 pm Hi, I am (obviously) a new poster, so I hope I'm not asking a previous question... Anyway, I am a long time Eva fan and I've been collecting basically everything for years now. Back in 98 or 97, I'm not sure of which, I picked up a set of books entitled "Evangelion Original" volumes I, II, and III. These have been out of print for years, and I have never found anyone who knew what they were or had even heard of them. I've been out of the Eva community loop for some time, and just happened upon this place, and thought I would let you guys have a crack at it. Anyway, the books each feature a pilot and Eva on the Dust Jacket, with a cross containing the title behind that. They are officially licensed GAiNAX products, and were printed by Fujimishobo in 1996. Being a college freshman taking Elementary Japanese I am only able to discern that they are either scripts or a novelized version of the show. Anyway, I've never seen these listed on any sites merchandise section, and the only person I knew that had seen them before was TheRossMan, from Ikari Gendo's Eva Faqs, and that was eight years ago. If any of you would like to read them or see what they look like, I could scan them. If these are common knowledge and I am making a fool of myself, I apologize.

[]: Re: Evangelion Original             Quote Post by Soluzar » Fri Jul 15, 2005 2:19 pm bph wrote: Hi, I am (obviously) a new poster, so I hope I'm not asking a previous question... Anyway, I am a long time Eva fan and I've been collecting basically everything for years now. Back in 98 or 97, I'm not sure of which, I picked up a set of books entitled "Evangelion Original" volumes I, II, and III. These have been out of print for years, and I have never found anyone who knew what they were or had even heard of them. I've been out of the Eva community loop for some time, and just happened upon this place, and thought I would let you guys have a crack at it. Anyway, the books each feature a pilot and Eva on the Dust Jacket, with a cross containing the title behind that. They are officially licensed GAiNAX products, and were printed by Fujimishobo in 1996. Being a college freshman taking Elementary Japanese I am only able to discern that they are either scripts or a novelized version of the show. Anyway, I've never seen these listed on any sites merchandise section, and the only person I knew that had seen them before was TheRossMan, from Ikari Gendo's Eva Faqs , and that was eight years ago. If any of you would like to read them or see what they look like, I could scan them. If these are common knowledge and I am making a fool of myself, I apologize.

[]:             Quote Post by bph » Fri Jul 15, 2005 2:25 pm Cool. I stowed these away years ago and just remembered I owned them in class one day; I never thought to compare them to the scripts in the Renewal set. So anyway, thanks for the info! Oh, in regard to that site, it was kind of the only thing around way back when, and I've done quite a bit more research in regard to Eva over the years, I assure you. The last time I regularly posted on an Eva message board, this place (and EvaOtaku) didn't exist, so I'm kind of behind on what has went down recently.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Aug 03, 2005 4:25 pm I have all three of the Evangelion Original books. And as I understand, they're just the scripts for the show. They were a tad of dissapointing purchase. No pictures to look at, just text. They may be out of print, but like many Eva books, but they can be found on second hand vendors. In fact, I see one site that has all three, for about 7 dollars each, which is about cover price with shipping tacked on. So it's not so bad. They're still available... infrequently... and for reasonable prices. Shipping takes 1-2 weeks, but what do you want? It is available. Collecting and importing Eva books is one of my guilty habits. :oops: Opening a box fresh from Japan is one the simpler joys of life.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Sun Aug 07, 2005 2:50 pm These books are curious. They contain the scripts allright, but not the precise scripts used on the series. They contain scenes not appearing on the show, and lack some that do appear. For example, we know for sure that the uniform turned into plugsuit in #20 was a mistake because the pseudo-substance line didn't exist there, same as that Keel line calling to imbed a soul on Lilith that appears on the book for #24 that doesn't appear on the real #24.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Aug 07, 2005 10:54 pm 0_0 what are exactly these books? Just transcriptions?

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Mon Aug 08, 2005 12:12 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: 0_0 what are exactly this books? Just transcription?

They contain scenes not appearing on the show, and lack some that do appear. For example, we know for sure that the uniform turned into plugsuit in #20 was a mistake because the pseudo-substance line didn't exist there, same as that Keel line calling to imbed a soul on Lilith that appears on the book for #24 that doesn't appear on the real #24.
